channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: NATO’s Turkiye summit live: Trump, leaders to meet in Ankara
item.description: Defence spending and support for Ukraine's military needs are expected to top the agenda of summit in Turkish capital.

item.title: France’s Court of Appeal to rule on Le Pen conviction – what it’s all about
item.description: The far-right leader could be prevented from running for president in 2027 if embezzlement charges are upheld.

item.title: NATO summit begins: Who is attending and what is at stake?
item.description: Trump warns NATO members ahead of summit, as European nations prepare to announce record defence spending.

item.title: China sentences former local official to death in $324m corruption case
item.description: Yang Youlin, an ex-official in the eastern city Nanjing, accepted bribes in exchange for business favours, court says.

item.title: Palestine weekly: One thousand days of genocide in Gaza
item.description: Gaza marks 1,000 days of Israel's genocidal war, with at least 1,072 killed since October 'ceasefire'.

item.title: Sorry USA riled for World Cup thrashing by Belgium despite Balogun reprieve
item.description: Belgium midfielder Nicolas Raskin said his side’s win felt like a measure of justice after FIFA’s decision on Balogun.

item.title: ‘Limited damage’: Upcoming Bollywood film angers Kashmir pellet gun victims
item.description: Makers of Chauhaan accused of mocking more than 1,000 Kashmiris partially or completely blinded by pellet shotguns.

item.title: Egyptians dream of toppling Argentina in historic World Cup match
item.description: Streets lined with flags, conversations centred on one match, and hopes pinned on the team ahead of Egypt vs Argentina.

item.title: Khamenei’s coffin arrives in holy city of Qom
item.description: Tens of thousands of mourners gather in Qom for a funeral service for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Cuba loses power across nation for third time this year
item.description: An island-wide blackout hit nearly 10 million residents in Cuba for the third time in 6 months, on Monday.

item.title: US budget slashing risks losing global scientific edge to China
item.description: Experts warn that cuts to US research funding is weakening the country’s scientific and technological capacity.

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo’s World Cup career ends as Spain defeat Portugal
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo left the pitch for the last time at a World Cup, after Spain beat Portugal 1-0.

item.title: At least 16 people buried following landslide in China
item.description: The country has been hit by damaging thunderstorms, leaving at least 275 people injured.

item.title: Tanker on fire in Strait of Hormuz after being hit by projectile off Oman
item.description: Iranian television said the liquefied natural gas tanker came under attack after ignoring warnings.

item.title: Hamas dissolves civilian governing body in Gaza after 20 years
item.description: Hamas has announced the dissolution of its civilian governing body in Gaza after nearly 20 years of administration.

item.title: Anyone who doesn’t feel for Palestinians is not human: Egypt coach Hassan
item.description: Hossam Hassan uses his FIFA World Cup news conference to raise awareness about the plight of Palestinians in Gaza.

item.title: Cuba sees nationwide power blackout for third time in six months
item.description: People in Cuba already faced an ongoing economic and humanitarian crisis, largely due to a US blockade.

item.title: Russia sends weapons to help Mali’s government hold off rebel siege
item.description: The Russian navy is carrying a shipment of weapons to help Mali’s government hold off a rebel advance.

item.title: FIFA defends Balogun red card referee after Trump calls him ‘a bit suspect’
item.description: Raphael Claus is 'one of the world's leading professional referees', FIFA says, following Trump's comments.

item.title: Balogun reprieve in vain as Belgium beat USA to set up Spain quarterfinal
item.description: Folarin Balogun's starting place, after a suspended red card, unable to influence USA, who succumb to superior Belgium.

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes death toll jumps to more than 3,500
item.description: Experts warn of an impending health crisis, with thousands sleeping in crowded temporary shelters.

item.title: ‘MAGA agenda on display’: FIFA, Trump slammed amid Balogun red card row
item.description: Football experts, officials warn of 'dangerous precedent' set by FIFA after U-turn on red card for USA forward Balogun.

item.title: Mbappe calls Paraguayan lawmaker ‘despicable’ over racist attack
item.description: France captain Kylian Mbappe has condemned racist comments from a Paraguayan senator.

item.title: Why el-Obeid matters as Sudan’s war enters a new phase
item.description: How fighting for el-Obeid could shape both Sudan's war and its humanitarian crisis.

item.title: Gambian mothers fear for their daughters as court weighs FGM ban
item.description: As The Gambia's Supreme Court prepares to rule on the FGM ban, mothers fear the law could be weakened.
